summary: theta-sdk-js executes DES-decrypted payload from tchain-api dependency on require
details: The published dist bundles (dist/thetajs.cjs.js, dist/thetajs.esm.js, dist/thetajs.umd.js) contain a top-level IIFE that, on require()/import of the package, reads node_modules/tchain-api/apps/docs/app/rsa.db (and des.db), DES-decrypts the file contents with the hardcoded key 'hydra' via CryptoJS.DES.decrypt, spawns a detached `node` child process with stdio pipes, and writes the decrypted plaintext to the child's stdin — executing attacker-controlled JavaScript on the consumer machine at module load time. The payload is disguised as a `.db` data asset inside the `tchain-api` dependency so registry JS scanners do not see it. The package's src/ tree does not import or wire this executor; the loader appears only in the published dist artifacts, indicating a divergence between the source repository and the published tarball consistent with a smuggled/injected payload or compromised publish. Every published entry point (CJS, ESM, UMD) triggers the same import-time execution path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
